summary: Wallet/credential stealer with hardcoded C2 in eth-wallet-sentinel
details: The package name advertises an Ethereum wallet security tool, and the shipped code matches the wallet/credential-stealer fingerprint. scanner.js contains hardcoded C2 endpoints at https://ddjidd564.github.io (lines 161 and 323) used via https.request/https.get with POST, alongside execSync of host-recon commands (whoami, os.hostname()) and curl invocations (lines 298, 323, 408) — the classic system-intel + filesystem read + HTTPS POST exfiltration shape. wallet-scanner.js further enumerates the filesystem with multiple POST sinks (lines 4, 723, 795, 871) and curl/ping calls (lines 626, 799, 1204, 1229, 1314), consistent with crypto-wallet/seed-phrase scanning and exfiltration. build-verifier.js executes execSync('docker push...') (line 210) together with hardcoded https.request POSTs (lines 315, 318, 777, 778) and filesystem enumeration. The combination of (a) a hardcoded attacker-controlled C2 (a github.io page used as a beacon target rather than as static hosting), (b) child_process exec of host-recon commands, (c) filesystem enumeration paired with HTTPS POST, and (d) a package name themed around wallet 'sentinel' functionality (lure for crypto users) is an unambiguous credential/wallet-stealer pattern.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
